message:
test(franken-node): harden Node.js detection in compat harness to reject Bun shims

Improve the is_real_node() detection in the FrankenNode compatibility
harness to reliably distinguish real Node.js from Bun's node shim in
CI/worker images:

- Tighten --version parsing: verify output starts with 'v' followed by
  an ASCII digit (previously only checked starts_with('v'))
- Add secondary probe using process.release.name, process.versions.node,
  and process.versions.bun to positively identify genuine Node.js
  (expects "node:true:false" — Bun would report "bun:..." or "node:true:true")
- Return false early if version check fails, avoiding the secondary probe

This prevents false-positive harness runs where Bun is masquerading as
Node, which would produce misleading conformance results for the
FrankenNode extension-host drop-in claim tier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
